title: Network Delay Time
problem_statement: Calculate how long it takes for all nodes to receive a signal sent from a source in a weighted directed graph.

examples.explanation: All nodes receive the signal by time 2.

intuition: Find shortest paths from the source to all nodes using Dijkstra, then take the maximum distance.

approaches.name: Dijkstra’s shortest path
approaches.description: Use a min-heap to pick the next closest node and update neighbor distances until all reachable nodes are processed.
approaches.why_not: Bellman-Ford works but is slower for this dense edge range.

interview_qa.q: What does the final answer represent?
interview_qa.a: The time when the farthest reachable node receives the signal.
Because edge weights are non-negative and we need shortest paths from one source.
